summary:
The common sign of occlusal trauma includes resorption of the alveolar ridge as this indicates long-standing issues from overloading teeth; while other symptoms may be present, bone loss provides clear evidence of sustained damage leading to structural change within the jawbone itself. To directly address your requirement for brevity:

The most reliable indication of occlusal trauma is \(\boxed{\text{resorption of the alveolar ridge}}\) because it reflects persistent mechanical forces that lead to irreversible tissue alteration. Summary ends here. Agent2 concludes by highlighting why resorption stands out clinically as an unmistakable marker of such dental injuries caused by improper bite alignment or pressure distribution. ```plaintext
Resorption of the alveolar ridge is the strongest indicator of occlusal trauma since it shows permanent bone loss resulting from extended periods of abnormal biting pressures.
```
